title: _elementtree: XMLParser._setevents iterates a caller-supplied list with unlocked PySequence_Fast_GET_ITEM, racing list_resize on a concurrently-appended shared list
description: _setevents calls PySequence_Fast(events_to_report), which returns a real list argument unchanged (no copy), then reads ob_item[i] via PySequence_Fast_GET_ITEM with no per-object lock. A concurrent list.append on the same shared list runs list_resize (memcpy + atomic republish of ob_item), racing the read. The racing state is the user's plain Python list, not the parser's internals -- the shared-list-mutation class surfaced through _setevents.
sites: Modules/_elementtree.c _setevents_impl:4209:36 (READ ob_item[i] via PySequence_Fast_GET_ITEM(events_seq, i); events_seq aliases the caller's list)
Modules/_elementtree.c _setevents_impl:4208 (READ Py_SIZE via PySequence_Fast_GET_SIZE in the loop guard)
Objects/listobject.c list_resize:160 (WRITE memcpy old->new ob_item buffer -- the seeded vehicle's write face)
Objects/listobject.c list_resize:165 (atomic WRITE _Py_atomic_store_ptr_release(&self->ob_item) -- this repro's write face)
Objects/listobject.c list_resize:169 (free_list_items(old_items, _PyObject_GC_IS_SHARED(self)); QSBR-deferred free for shared lists)
Modules/_elementtree.c _setevents_impl:4187:5 (self/self face: Py_XSETREF(target->events_append, ...) unlocked refcount RMW when one parser is shared)
race_kind: shared-list mutated concurrently (unlocked PySequence_Fast_GET_ITEM read vs list_resize); related self/self face = unsynchronized shared-parser TreeBuilder state
shared_state: the caller-supplied events_to_report list (PyListObject.ob_item buffer); for the self/self face: XMLParser.target (TreeBuilder) events_append / *_event_obj fields
severity: low (assigned list_resize face is crash-safe in practice -- old ob_item buffer QSBR-deferred for shared lists -- and is user-side concurrent mutation of a passed-in list; the self/self face is more serious refcount RMW but needs a shared parser)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66 on every run; 2/2 confirmation runs)
suggested_fix: caller-side: don't mutate the events list from another thread while a parser consumes it. Optional _elementtree hardening: snapshot with PySequence_List(events_to_report) (or Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION on events_seq) instead of aliasing the caller's live list via PySequence_Fast + fast macros.
assessment: NOT standalone. list_resize/atomic faces = the TSAN-0013 shared-list class surfaced through _setevents (PySequence_Fast aliases the caller's live list, unlocked read). self/self face = TSAN-0009 don't-share-the-parser class (Py_XSETREF/Py_CLEAR refcount RMW). Signatures folded into 0013/0009.
notes: _setevents is the internal path of the public XMLPullParser(events=...) and iterparse(source, events=...). PySequence_Fast returns a list/tuple arg as-is (Py_NewRef, no copy), so events_seq aliases the caller's live list; PySequence_Fast_GET_ITEM is the unlocked PyList_GET_ITEM macro. Verified the race is NOT parser-internal on this face by giving each reader its own XMLParser. The self/self face was separately confirmed (SUMMARY _elementtree.c:4187:5, exit 66) with one shared parser + thread-local events lists.
